Japanese: ２月２４日　某時刻 検事局・地下駐車場
English: February 24 Prosecutor's Office Underground Parking Lot

Japanese: 今日は、ダレもいないね。 ‥‥おキョウさんも。
English: No one's here today... Not even Ms. Starr.

Japanese: 証拠保管室のナゾが キョーレツでしたからねー。
English: Everyone's probably busy looking into what exactly went down in the evidence room.

Japanese: 刑事さんたち、あっちに 行ってるんじゃないかなあ。
English: That must be where the detectives are.

Japanese: でも、今日の法廷で ハッキリ立証したからね。
English: But we proved in court today that no one was murdered in the evidence room at 5:15 PM,

Japanese: 《事件当日の５時１５分、 証拠保管室で、事件はなかった》
English: on the day of the crime.

Japanese: それって、なんか‥‥１歩、 前進したような気がするケド。
English: Yeah. I thought we were finally making some headway in our case!

Japanese: ケッキョク、ともえさんが アヤシイ、ってコトになるかな。
English: But instead it looks like we just ended up making Lana look even more guilty.

Japanese: うううう‥‥
English: Hang in there, Lana...

Japanese: （明日までに‥‥すべて、 コタエを見つけないと！）
English: (I've got to find all the answers by tomorrow!)

Japanese: ここは‥‥クルマの出口ですね。
English: This is where the cars leave the lot.

Japanese: 床のヤジルシを見ると、 “入口”みたいだけどね。
English: The arrow on the ground makes it look more like an entrance.

Japanese: ナニ言ってるんですか！ どう見ても出口じゃないですか！
English: What are you talking about? It's plainly an exit!

Japanese: ま、まあ‥‥出口であると同時に、 入口でもある、というコトかな。
English: Well, maybe it's both. Kind of a dual purpose?

Japanese: あ！　それ！　あの有名な “ソータイセー理論”ですね！
English: Ah hah! The theory of relativity!

Japanese: え！　は、はあ‥‥
English: What? Uh...

Japanese: メモしとかないとね‥‥ あ！　ね。ね。成歩堂さん。
English: I've got to write this down. Ah! Hey, hey, Mr. Wright! Maybe you know...

Japanese: どこの国のヒトでしたっけ？ ソー・タイセーさんって。
English: Was Mr. Relativity, German? Or was he British?

Japanese: ‥‥知らないよ。
English: Mr. Relativity? Are you sure that was his name?

Japanese: あ。ハシゴですよ。
English: Ah hah! A ladder.

Japanese: それはキャタツだよ。
English: Um... That's a stepladder.

Japanese: どこが違うんですか？ カガク的に証明してください。
English: What's the difference? In scientific terms, please.

Japanese: か、カガク的‥‥
English: S-scientific huh?

Japanese: もっとホンシツを見ましょうよ、 成歩堂さん。
English: Look at the basic nature of the thing, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: （なぜだろう‥‥あまずっぱい キモチになってきた）
English: (This all seems so horribly familiar somehow...)

Japanese: お。こんなところに イミありげなドアが！
English: Look! A door! This must mean something!

Japanese: ドアに“イミ”なんてないよ。
English: I'm not sure that doors "mean" anything.

Japanese: クッ！　開きません！ ナゾのカギがかかってます！
English: No! It won't open! A mysterious lock!

Japanese: カギに“ナゾ”なんてないよ。
English: I fail to see what's mysterious about it.

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂さん。 もっと人生を楽しみましょうよ。
English: Mr. Wright... You need to learn to enjoy life more.

Japanese: 先に調査をすませようよ。
English: Let's finish our investigation first, shall we?

Japanese: おキョウさん‥‥ あそこから見たんですね‥‥事件。
English: So that's where Ms. Starr saw the incident from.

Japanese: 高いぶん、フツウよりも よく見えたかもしれないね。
English: You can probably see quite a lot from up there.

Japanese: （そうなると‥‥）
（“彼女が刺したのを見た”と いう証言も、ウソとは思えない）
English: (That probably means she wasn't lying when she saw Ms. Skye stab him...)

Japanese: そもそも‥‥警備員は ナニをしてたんだろう。
English: Where's the security guard, anyway?

Japanese: ええと。あたしが 聞いてきたトコロによると‥‥
English: Well, this is just something I heard...

Japanese: どうやら、おキョウさんのお茶を 買いに行ってたみたいです！
English: But apparently he went out to buy coffee for Ms. Starr.

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (That woman is a force to be reckoned with...)

Japanese: これが‥‥モンダイの ドラム缶だね。
English: So, this is the famous oil drum.

Japanese: ちょっと、ケリ倒してみますね。 ここはひとつ、あたしも。
English: Well, no time like the present! I'll try to kick it over myself!

Japanese: えいっ！
English: Hii-yah!

Japanese: な、泣かないでほしいな。 （ともえさん‥‥スゴいパワーだ）
English: Th-that's okay. Don't cry. (That Lana Skye must be a powerful woman...)

Japanese: これが‥‥あの “ついたて”ですね。
English: So, this is the famous divider.

Japanese: おキョウさんの証言のウソを 立証してくれたカベ、だったな。
English: It sure helped us knock a hole in that testimony yesterday.

Japanese: あるイミ、このカベこそが ホンモノの証人だったのかもね。
English: Come to think of it, this divider helped our case more than the actual witness.

Japanese: 《カベに耳あり、正直メアリー》 ‥‥まさに、そんな感じですね。
English: The great divider! A chip off the old parking block!

Japanese: メアリーはないよ、あかねちゃん。 （今の子は知らないのかな、障子）
English: It's just a wall. (Scientifically speaking...)

Japanese: お。電話がありますよ。 ‥‥どれどれ。
English: Here, a phone. Let's see if it works...

Japanese: こらこら。 勝手にさわらないほうがいいよ。
English: Hey. Don't touch stuff we don't need to be touching.

Japanese: な、何も聞こえません！
English: I can't hear anything!

Japanese: あたしの耳、ヘンになったのカモ！ 気圧のせい、でしょうか‥‥
English: My ears! No, my ears! Maybe it's due to the barometric pressure...

Japanese: （ナニを、バカなコトを‥‥）
English: (What is she babbling about?)

Japanese: あ、そこ！　なんですか！
English: H-hello!? What did you just say?

Japanese: 聞こえてるじゃないか！ 電話がコワれてるんだよ！
English: Look, you can hear just fine. The phone's broken!

Japanese: まっかなすぽーつかーですね。
English: That's about as red as a sports car can get.

Japanese: ‥‥そうだね。ジツにまっかだ。
English: Yup, it's pretty red alright.

Japanese: （アイツのクルマから 被害者の死体が発見された）
English: (The body was found in Edgeworth's car trunk...)

Japanese: （トランクのカギも コワされている）
English: (And the lock on the trunk was broken.)

Japanese: （ともえさんは‥‥なぜ、 アレを選んだのか‥‥）
English: (So, the question is why did Ms. Skye choose his car?)

Japanese: で？　で？　なんていう クルマなんですか？　アレ。
English: So, what model car is it?

Japanese: “セダン”か“クーペ”だと 思うんだ。クルマの名前なら。
English: I think it was called a "Sedan" or a "Coupe." Something like that.

Japanese: ‥‥それ、車種です。 成歩堂さん、オトコの子でしょ！
English: Those are car types, Mr. Wright. Not models.
You're a guy, aren't you, Mr. Wright? You're supposed to know these things!

Japanese: （この事件が解決したら、 免許をとろう‥‥）
English: (Maybe it is about time I got my driver's license...)

Japanese: 《Ａブロック》‥‥ 検察関係者のエリアですね。
English: "A Block"... This area is reserved for prosecutors.

Japanese: ぼくは弁護士だから、 《Ｂブロック》になるのかな。
English: Defense attorneys are relegated to "B Block."

Japanese: いつかあたし、ここに クルマをとめるのがユメなんです！
English: I dream of the day when I will be able to park my car here!

Japanese: それで、Ｂブロックの成歩堂さん から、ハンバーグ弁当を買うの。
English: I'll go over to B Block to buy my hamburgers from you, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥とうぶん、弁護士を やめるつもりはないよ。
English: I'm not giving up my job that soon.

Japanese: この向こうが《Ｂブロック》 ‥‥外来者用のエリアだね。
English: "B Block" is through there. That's where visitors park.

Japanese: おキョウさん‥‥ この金網を乗り越えたんですか！
English: So... Ms. Starr climbed over this fence!?

Japanese: そ、そうみたいだね。 （３メートル以上あるぞ‥‥）
English: I-it seems so, yeah. (That fence is 9 feet high at least!)

Japanese: ちょっと、のぼってみますね。 ここはひとつ、あたしも。
English: Well no time like the present! I think I'll give it a little try!

Japanese: きゃあっ！
English: Eeeek!

Japanese: だ。だから、泣かないでほしいな。 （おキョウさん‥‥さすがだな）
English: I-it's okay, don't cry. (Maybe there's a Lunchland Olympics team?)

Japanese: このロープ‥‥やっぱり？
English: This rope... is it...?

Japanese: うん。被害者の死体のカタチを ロープでなぞってあるんだよ。
English: Yep. They laid it in the outline of the victim's body.
So wait...

Japanese: 被害者‥‥トランクを閉めるとき、 挟まって死んだのでしょうか！
English: The victim must have died when the killer closed the trunk on him!

Japanese: （こんな発想をする子、 他にはゼッタイいないだろうな）
English: (You have got to be the only person I know that would come to that conclusion.)

Japanese: ここにあったんですよね。 ‥‥このメモ。
English: That's where we found that note.
The SL-9 Incident.

Japanese: 事件があった日、《申し送り》に なるはずだった事件。
English: A case that was due for transferal the day of the murder.

Japanese: まさか、それが‥‥ あの事件だった、なんて‥‥
English: I never knew... The "SL-9 incident" referred to...

Japanese: 《青影事件》‥‥か。
English: The Joe Darke Killings.

Japanese: （今回の事件の関係者が全員、 何かの形で関わっている事件）
English: (Everyone in this case played some part in that incident.)

Japanese: （ゼッタイに‥‥ グーゼンなんかじゃない！）
English: (This has to be more than just sheer coincidence!)

Japanese: ２月２４日　某時刻 検事局・地下駐車場
English: February 24 Prosecutor's Office Underground Parking Lot

Japanese: ‥‥そちら‥‥
English: Excuse me...

Japanese: 最高級和牛のシンタマ、 ９０グラムでよろしかったかしら。
English: Would either of you care for a quarter pound of roast beef?

Japanese: お、おキョウさん！ （すでに弁当ですらないな）
English: Ms. Starr! (I guess she's out of lunches.)

Japanese: あなたたちも、よほどのモノ好きで いらっしゃるコトですわねぇ‥‥
English: You certainly are the curious sort, aren't you?

Japanese: まるで‥‥そう。この世で初めて ナットウを口にした殿方のごとく。
English: Kind of like the first person who sucked a cow's nipple to discover milk.

Japanese: あの‥‥２年前の事件まで レンジであたためなおすとは‥‥
English: Still, I never thought you'd go digging up that case from two years ago.

Japanese: 《ＳＬ９号事件》は‥‥関係者 すべての名前が登場する事件です。
English: Everyone in this trial was involved in the SL-9 Incident.

Japanese: それが《申し送り》によって完全に 終わる日、今回の事件が起こった。
English: Not only that, but the murder occurred on the very day the evidence from that case was

Japanese: グーゼンとは思えません。
English: due for transferal. This can't all be attributed to mere "coincidence."

Japanese: ワタクシの見た、あの光景‥‥ よもや、お忘れじゃないでしょう？
English: Aren't you forgetting something?
You know, that little scene I happened to witness?

Japanese: （ともえさんが、多田敷捜査官に ナイフを突き立てた、瞬間‥‥）
English: (The instant Lana stabbed Detective Goodman with a knife.)

Japanese: 過去のヒミツを暴いても‥‥ その瞬間を変えるコトは、不可能。
English: No matter how much of the past you dig up, it won't change what I saw.

Japanese: ソテーにしたシンタマは、味わって 飲みくだすしかないんだよ。
English: Roast beef is meant to be savored when eaten.

Japanese: （おキョウさんの、ともえさんに 対する“悪意”‥‥）
English: (Ms. Starr's hatred toward Lana...)

Japanese: （すべては、やはり‥‥ ２年前に始まっているんだ！）
English: (It all dates back to two years ago!)

Japanese: 青影　丈‥‥その名前は、 今も忘れちゃいないさ。
English: Joe Darke... That's a name I'll not soon forget!

Japanese: ヒドい緊張とプレッシャーの下で、 アイツを追っていた、半年間。
English: We trailed him for half a year. Oh, the pressure!

Japanese: 今、思えば。あれほど充実していた 日々はなかったねぇ。
English: Still, I don't think I was ever more alive than I was then.

Japanese: ひたすら熱く、濃く煮つめた グレービーソースのような毎日‥‥
English: Those days were steamier than a bowl of hot gravy!

Japanese: もっとも‥‥罪門　恭介にとっては ジゴクのような日々だったろうが。
English: Poor old Jake Marshall, though, must have been going through hell.

Japanese: それは‥‥やっぱり、 弟さんの“死”ですか。
English: You mean, because of his brother's death?

Japanese: ‥‥罪門兄弟は、それは仲が よかったからねぇ。
English: They were close, those two.

Japanese: ナオトが死んでからのキョウスケの 執念‥‥見ていて恐ろしかった。
English: After Neil died, something took over Jake. He became obsessed.

Japanese: そんなアイツを見て‥‥あの女も ほっておけなかったんだろうさ。
English: Seeing Jake like that made her all the more desperate.

Japanese: あの女‥‥？
宝月　巴だよ。
English: Lana Skye.

Japanese: お‥‥‥お姉ちゃん、ですか‥‥
English: My sister...?

Japanese: 《ＳＬ９号事件》には、最高の 捜査班が組まれたモノさ。
English: The best of the best were put on that SL-9 case.

Japanese: 当然‥‥その指揮には、 伝説のコンビがあたった。
English: Of course they were lead by that legendary duo.

Japanese: お姉ちゃんと‥‥巌徒局長！
English: Lana... and Chief Gant!

Japanese: 巌徒　海慈に、宝月　巴‥‥
English: Damon Gant and Lana Skye...

Japanese: ２年前は、主席捜査官と、副主席。 警察捜査のトップだった。
English: Two years ago Gant was Chief Detective, and Lana second in command. They were the best.

Japanese: ふたりで、数々の難事件を カイケツしたんですよね！
English: They solved all kinds of cases together, didn't they?

Japanese: ‥‥特に、巌徒　海慈の磁力は、 異常なほどだったねぇ。
English: Damon Gant's magnetism in particular was almost unreal.
His "magnetism"?

Japanese: “証拠品”を 引きつけるチカラだよ。
English: By that I mean his ability to attract evidence.

Japanese: 彼が扱った事件では‥‥不自然な ほど、意外な証拠が出たものさ。
English: He'd produce the most incredible evidence in the cases he handled.

Japanese: （“意外な証拠”‥‥まさか！）
English: ("Incredible evidence"? You mean...)

Japanese: そう。そのころから、 ある種の“ギワク”はあった。
English: Oh, yes. There were rumors about him even back then.

Japanese: ‥‥ダレも、口には 出せなかったけどね。
English: No one dared confront him, though.

Japanese: （“ねつ造”だろうな ‥‥言うまでもなく）
English: (I take it she's talking about forged evidence.)

Japanese: あのころの宝月　巴は、 捜査官全員のアコガレだったよ。
English: Back then, everyone looked up to Lana. All the detectives wanted to be like her.

Japanese: ！　そうだったんですか？
English: ! Really?

Japanese: このおキョウも、そのひとりさ。 ‥‥バカみたいだけどねェ。
English: Oh yes... myself included. I was a fool, really.

Japanese: 曲がったコトがキライで、いつも 捜査官のコトを考えてくれていた。
English: She hated anything crooked, and always watched out for the other detectives.

Japanese: だから‥‥あの事件でも、 キョウスケのコトを気にしてたよ。
English: That's why she was so concerned for Jake.

Japanese: 罪門さんを‥‥
English: Mr. Marshall...

Japanese: 弟を殺害された苦しみを、 自分のコトのように感じていたね。
English: When Jake's brother was murdered, she felt as if she had lost her own brother.

Japanese: キョウスケが、弟の死に耐えられた のは、彼女のおかげだった。
English: If it wasn't for her, I don't think Jake would ever have recovered from his shock.

Japanese: ‥‥ちょっと、クヤシイけどね。
English: That's what makes it all the more infuriating.

Japanese: おキョウさん‥‥
English: Ms. Starr...

Japanese: だからこそ‥‥よけいに、 あの女がユルせないのさ。
English: That's why... I'll never be able to forgive her.

Japanese: どうして。あんなふうに、 冷たい女に変わっちまったのか‥‥
English: Why did she have to turn so cold after that...?

Japanese: 《伝説のコンビ》を信じていたから ‥‥キビしい捜査をつづけられた。
English: That legendary pair was the reason we were able to keep up our investigation.

Japanese: それだけに、あの事件の結末は あまりにショックだったね‥‥
English: That's why we're so shocked over how it turned out...

Japanese: 証拠品の“ねつ造”‥‥ですか。
English: You mean, with the forging of the evidence?

Japanese: たしかに、青影　丈は ムクイを受けた。‥‥それはいい。
English: Don't get me wrong. Joe Darke got what he deserved.

Japanese: でも！　法廷に提出された証拠は、 あきらかに“操作”されていた！
English: Still, it was obvious the evidence produced in court was being manipulated.

Japanese: 捜査班の知らない証拠や‥‥ 逆に、提出されなかった証拠。
English: Items our team never found would suddenly appear, while other items were kept secret.

Japanese: でも。ジッサイに 不正があったという証拠は‥‥
English: But you didn't have proof anything illegal was done...

Japanese: このおキョウを見れば‥‥ 何があったか、アキラカだろうさ。
English: I'm proof enough of what happened.

Japanese: 事件のあと‥‥多田敷をのぞいた 捜査官たちは、その任を解かれた。
English: After that case, all of us save Goodman were relieved of our duties...

Japanese: ‥‥ほとんど、なんの説明もなく。
English: most without even so much as an explanation.

Japanese: そして‥‥宝月　巴は、主席検事 として、検事局に移転したのさ。
English: Then Lana Skye transferred to the Prosecutor's Office and became Chief Prosecutor.

Japanese: お姉ちゃん‥‥ムカシから、 検事を志望していたから。
English: Lana always wanted to be a prosecutor.

Japanese: ‥‥コトは、そう タンジュンじゃないよ。
English: Nothing's quite as simple as it appears.
Huh?

Japanese: 宝月　巴は‥‥操られていた。 おキョウは、そうニラんでるねェ。
English: Lana Skye was merely being used as a pawn. That's my take on the matter.

Japanese: “操られていた”‥‥‥？
English: She was being used?

Japanese: ともえさんが移転したのは‥‥ ２年前、だったそうですね。
English: Lana transferred to the Prosecutor's Office two years ago, didn't she?

Japanese: ‥‥そう。巌徒《局長》の、 強力な後押しのおかげで、ね。
English: Yes, thanks to "Chief" Gant's powerful influence.
Chief...

Japanese: 《ＳＬ９号事件》を解決した功績で 巌徒は、警察局のトップに立った。
English: That's right. Having solved the SL-9 case, his position as chief was secured.

Japanese: その彼が、次に狙うエモノは‥‥ 当然、ひとつしかない。
English: There was only one thing left for him to control, and then no one could stand in
his way:

Japanese: ‥‥検事局さ。
English: the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: ！　ま、まさか‥‥ そのために、ともえさんを！
English: What? You mean... That's why Lana was transferred!?

Japanese: 《主席検事》を押さえれば、事実上 検事局を動かすことができる。
English: If he could control the Chief Prosecutor, he could control the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: ‥‥それが、巌徒　海慈の 狙いだったんだろうねェ‥‥
English: That must have been his goal all along.

Japanese: でも！　なぜ、お姉ちゃんが 局長さんの言いなりに‥‥
English: B-but how could he control Lana?

Japanese: それは、わからないさ。‥‥でも。
English: I don't know, but one thing's for sure.

Japanese: 彼女が“変わった”のは、 あの事件が終わってから、だ。
English: Ever since that case ended, she's never been the same.

Japanese: そこに“何か”ある‥‥ そう考えるべきじゃないかねェ。
English: It's only logical to conclude...
there must have been a reason for her change.

Japanese: （どうやら‥‥）
English: (At last...)

Japanese: （やっと、この事件のポイントが 見えてきたような気がするな）
English: (I'm finally getting close to the bottom of this ugly mess.)

Japanese: ‥‥ありがとうこざいます。 おキョウさん。
English: Thank you, Ms. Starr.

Japanese: ボウヤ‥‥
English: You listen to me, Rookie.

Japanese: 食材を活かすも殺すも、 料理のウデしだい、さ。
English: It takes more than just ingredients to create fine cuisine.

Japanese: ‥‥アンタに、この情報を 活かすコトができるかしらね‥‥
English: I hope you turn out to be a better chef than I've been...

Japanese: ‥‥今さら、ボウヤにあげる 情報など、ございませんわ。
English: I don't have anything else to tell you.

Japanese: おキョウさん‥‥
English: Ms. Starr...

Japanese: ワタクシにあげられるのは‥‥ ゴマシオ弁当ぐらいかしら。
English: The only thing I can give you now, is a poppyseed rice set.

Japanese: （安いな、コイツは‥‥）
English: (Talk about cheap...)

Japanese: ゴマシオを食べて‥‥明日は シロクロ、ハッキリすることね。
English: Eat this, and maybe you'll be able to tell black from white in court tomorrow.

Japanese: ２月２４日　某時刻 上級検事執務室・１２０２号
English: February 24 High Prosecutor's Office Room 1202

Japanese: ‥‥いませんね。御剣検事さん。
English: Mr. Edgeworth isn't here.

Japanese: また、査問会で シボられてるのかな。
English: Maybe he's being questioned by an inquiry committee?

Japanese: （今日もヒドかったからな ‥‥法廷）
English: (He took a real beating in court today...)

Japanese: お姉ちゃん、認めちゃいました からね‥‥２年前の、不正。
English: Yeah, with Lana admitting to falsifying evidence two years ago...

Japanese: ‥‥とにかく、 またあとで来てみようか。
English: I guess we'll just have to come back later.

Japanese: せっかくもらったのに、 ほったらかしですね。
English: He won such a prestigious award. Why would he just leave it on the couch?

Japanese: たしかに‥‥トノサマンを 置いておくよりはいいと思うけど。
English: Better this than that giant Steel Samurai.

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥アイツ、 ムカシからそうだったな）
English: (Edgeworth has always been like that.)

Japanese: え！　なんですか？ メモしますから、教えてください！
English: Really? Hey, I should write this down. Tell me what he was like as a kid!

Japanese: アイツ、小学生のころ、やたら いろんな賞をもらってたんだよ。
English: Back in elementary school, he won awards for all kinds of things.

Japanese: 弁論大会とか、論文とか、 フルートとか、ゴルフとか。
English: Speech contests, essays, playing the flute, golf tournaments...

Japanese: ‥‥どことなく、小学生っぽくない ラインナップですね。
English: That doesn't exactly sound like typical elementary school stuff.

Japanese: でも‥‥アイツ自身は、 賞をもらうのがキライだったんだ。
English: But he always hated receiving awards. He'd make some smart remark like,

Japanese: 「自分が歩いてきた道ならば、 自分自身が知っているのだよ」
English: "I know the path I've walked. No one else needs to tell me."

Japanese: ‥‥とか、なんとか。
English: That's how he's always been.

Japanese: ちょっぴり、ハラ立ちますね。
English: Kind of gets under your skin, doesn't it?

Japanese: ひゃあ！
English: Whoa!

Japanese: すごいながめですねー。 １２階でしたっけ。ここ。
English: What a view! It must be nice to have an office on the 12th floor.

Japanese: たしかに‥‥なんか、 エラくなった気分になるね。
English: I guess you would feel... important.

Japanese: ‥‥ちなみに。
English: Incidentally...

Japanese: ここから飛び降りたとき、 イノチを落とすカクリツは‥‥
English: Were you to jump out this window, your chance of fatality would be...

Japanese: 出ましたっ！ ９９．９９９８パーセントです！
English: Got it! 99.9998 percent!

Japanese: （‥‥０．０００２パーセントは 助かるのか‥‥）
English: (There's a 0.0002% chance you'd live?)

Japanese: シゴト机だ。さすが、 キレイに片づいているなあ。
English: A work desk. It's quite tidy, as one might expect.

Japanese: いいですねー、この机。 使いやすいけど、オシャレで。
English: What a nice desk! Easy to use, and easy on the eyes!

Japanese: カオが映るほど、よーく みがきこんでありますよ。
English: It's polished so high I can see my own reflection.

Japanese: （‥‥なぜだろう。イトノコ刑事の カオが浮かんだぞ‥‥）
English: (Strange... Why did I just picture Detective Gumshoe?)

Japanese: あのネームプレート、おみやげに 持って帰ろうかなあ。
English: Maybe I'll take that name plaque as a souvenir.

Japanese: やめたほうがいいよ。起訴される。
English: Don't. He'll sue you.

Japanese: うわあ‥‥これ、ゼンブ 事件のファイルですか！
English: Whoa! These are all case files!?

Japanese: 天井まで、ギッシリ詰まってます！ ハシゴもついていますね。
English: They're stacked up to the ceiling! There's even a ladder...

Japanese: ‥‥おかしいな。アイツ、 地震がニガテなはずだから‥‥
English: Odd...

Japanese: こんな危なっかしい本棚を 使うとは思えないんだけど。
English: I thought Edgeworth wasn't good with heights.

Japanese: だいじょうぶです！
English: He must have someone get them for him.

Japanese: ものすごく大きなボルトで、カベに メリこむほど固定されています！
すごいパワーだなあ。 御剣検事さんがやったのかなあ。
（‥‥なぜだろう。イトノコ刑事の カオが浮かんだぞ‥‥）
English: (Strange... Why did I just picture Detective Gumshoe?)

Japanese: でもさ。地震が起こったら、 部屋中にファイルの雨が降るね。
だいじょうぶです！
English: He must study these case reports so closely... He's so cool!

Japanese: ギュウギュウに詰まってますから、 ちょっとやそっとじゃ抜けません！
やっぱり、なんか おかしくない？　それ。
English: You wouldn't say that if you saw him sweating bullets up on that ladder.

Japanese: あ。チェスボードがありますよ！
English: Hey, a chessboard!

Japanese: チェスはよくわからないけど‥‥ 追いつめられてるね。青いコマが。
English: I'm not too up on my chess but it looks like Blue's in a bit of a tight spot.

Japanese: 赤いナイトたちが、よってたかって 青いポーンを取りかこんでいます。
English: The Red Knights have surrounded the Blue Pawn...

Japanese: ‥‥どういうこと？
English: Huh?

Japanese: “ナイト”は“剣を持った騎士” “ポーン”は“歩兵”です。
English: Those horses are mounted knights. Their swords have really sharp "edges!"
And check out that poor pawn, his head is kind of spiky... Kind of reminds me of you.

Japanese: 赤い“剣”が、青い“歩”を 追いつめている‥‥
御剣検事さん、チェス好きかあ。 ‥‥どうしました？　成歩堂さん。
English: Mr. Edgeworth must be an avid chess player! What's wrong, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: （気のせいだよな、きっと）
English: (Edges... surrounding a pawn with spiky hair... Nah... It's nothing.)

Japanese: きゃあ、カワイイ！ ステキなティーセットですよ！
English: Oooh! Cute! What a pretty tea set!

Japanese: ‥‥ぼく、ティーバッグ派だから。
English: I go more for the instant tea bags myself.

Japanese: すごい！　下のタナに、茶葉と缶が ギッシリ詰まってます！
English: Amazing! The drawer below is filled with packets of tea leaves!

Japanese: 産地別にキチンと並んでますよ。 フレバリーも、種類ごとに！
English: They're all sorted by place of origin and flavor!

Japanese: お。このロイヤル、ミゴトなまでに 暴力的なブレンディングです！
English: Look at this Royal blend! What an exquisitely splendid concoction!

Japanese: （‥‥シュミって、なんでも 奥が深いものなんだなあ‥‥）
English: (There's such a thing as taking a hobby too far...)

Japanese: わあ。ゴーカな花ですねェ。 御剣検事さんに、ピッタリ！
English: My, my, my! What an amazing bouquet! Just right for Mr. Edgeworth.

Japanese: たしかにね。‥‥あ。 カードが添えてあるぞ。
English: No kidding. Hey, there's a card on it...

Japanese: ‥‥《恐怖政治　　　カオル》
English: "To be or not to be -- Wendy"

Japanese: （“カオル”‥‥ どこかで聞いたような）
English: ("Wendy?"... I've heard that name somewhere before.)

Japanese: となりにあるのは‥‥ 巨大なトノサマンですっ！
English: And beside it... A giant Steel Samurai!

Japanese: うわ、ほしいなあ。 御剣検事さん、マニアなのかなあ。
English: Wow, I want one!

Japanese: イヤだな、それ。‥‥あ。 足のウラ、何か書いてあるぞ。
English: Huh? There's something written on the bottom of his foot.

Japanese: ‥‥《三割打者　　　カオル》
English: "To every cloud, there is a silver lining -- Wendy"

Japanese: なんか、きのうまでと モンクが変わってませんか？
English: Was this the same message that was here yesterday?

Japanese: さすが、御剣検事さん。 ふかふかですよ、ソファ！
English: Mr. Edgeworth has such a comfy sofa!

Japanese: こんなリッパなソファ、 座った瞬間に眠くなるよ。
English: Sofas like this make me want to curl up and take a nap.

Japanese: きっと、夜おそくまで 資料を調べて、つかれきって‥‥
English: I bet he pours over his case files here until the wee hours of the morning...

Japanese: 上着をぬいで、シャツのソデなんか オシャレにまくっちゃって。
English: Then he takes off his jacket, rolls up his sleeves...

Japanese: ウデまくらで、ついウトウト 寝ちゃうんですよねー。
English: And goes to sleep using his arms as a pillow!

Japanese: （彼女の目には、見えている らしいな‥‥オソロシイことに）
English: (I don't believe it. She's actually daydreaming about Edgeworth working...)

Japanese: 寝グセや、ホッペにソファの カドのアトがついちゃって。
English: I bet in the morning he has sofa hair, and little creases in his cheek from the seams!

Japanese: ‥‥カッコいいなあ。
English: He's so cool!

Japanese: ‥‥カッコいいかあ？
English: Sofa-hair is cool!?

Japanese: うわ！　なんか、いちだんと ヒラヒラした服ですよ！
English: Wow! This jacket is even lacier than his usual ones!

Japanese: いわゆる“勝負服”かな。 御剣検事さんの！
English: This must be his lucky trial jacket!

Japanese: 勝負服、ねえ‥‥。 ぼくは見たことないけどな。
English: Lucky jacket, right... I've never seen him wear it.

Japanese: 額に入れてかざっちゃって。 ナニか“いわく”がありそう！
English: I'm sure there's a story behind why it's in a frame!

Japanese: ちょっとシツレイして。 写真、撮っちゃお！
English: Maybe I'll be naughty... and take a picture!

Japanese: （やれやれ。 コーフンぎみだな‥‥）
English: (She's getting way too excited about this.)

Japanese: ２月２４日　某時刻 上級検事執務室・１２０２号
English: February 24 High Prosecutor's Office Room 1202

Japanese: （御剣‥‥戻ってるかな）
English: (I wonder if Edgeworth is back yet.)

Japanese: あ。いましたよ！ ‥‥何か、書いてます。
English: There he is! It looks like he's writing something.

Japanese: な、なんだ！　こんなところに‥‥
English: Huh? What are you doing here?

Japanese: （御剣のヤツ‥‥書いてたモノを あわてて床に捨てたぞ）
English: (He sure was quick to throw that paper on the floor...)

Japanese: タイヘンだったな。 ‥‥今日の法廷も。
English: Tough day in court, huh?

Japanese: ‥‥フッ。
English: Hmph.

Japanese: この２年間、私はつねに ギワクのキリに包まれてきた。
English: I've had to live the past two years with rumors flying around.

Japanese: 今さら、どうということもない。
English: What's another allegation to me?

Japanese: 元気出してくださいね！ あたし、オウエンしてますから！
English: Cheer up, Mr. Edgeworth! I'm rooting for you!

Japanese: （コイツは、 ムカシからそうだった‥‥）
English: (That's Edgeworth for you...)

Japanese: （‥‥本当に苦しいときこそ、 それをかくそうとする‥‥）
English: (Always trying to hide his real feelings.)

Japanese: それで‥‥なんの用だ？ こう見えて、私も忙しいのだが。
English: So, what do you want? Unlike some people, I don't have all day.

Japanese: せっかくもらったのに、 ほったらかしですね。
English: He won such a prestigious award. Why would he just leave it on the couch?

Japanese: たしかに‥‥トノサマンを 置いておくよりはいいと思うけど。
English: Better this than that giant Steel Samurai.

Japanese: どうしたの？
English: What is it?

Japanese: これ、《検事・オブ・ザ・イヤー》 の賞品ですよね？
English: This is the "King of Prosecutors" award, right?

Japanese: そうだよ。‥‥何を今さら。
English: Of course it is. You know that!

Japanese: なんか、今日。どこかで、 コレと似たものを見た気が‥‥
English: I saw something today that looked like this, somewhere...

Japanese: “似たもの”‥‥？
English: Something that looked like that?

Japanese: でも、どこかちがうような‥‥ なんだっけなあ、あれ。
English: Yeah, only different. What was it...?

Japanese: （たしかに‥‥何か、引っかかる。 アイツに見せてみるか‥‥？）
English: (She's right. Something's amiss here. Maybe I should show him a certain item...)

Japanese: （そういえば‥‥さっき アイツ、何を書いていたんだ？）
English: (I wonder what he was writing before?)

Japanese: ね。ちょっと調べてみましょうよ、 成歩堂さん！
English: Come on, Mr. Wright! Let's take a look!

Japanese: え！　‥‥やめとこうよ。 シュミが悪いし。
English: Are you crazy? Edgeworth is sitting right there!

Japanese: じゃ。検事さんの目をそらして くださいね。おねがいします！
English: Just distract him. I'll check it out.

Japanese: え‥‥！ え。ええと、御剣‥‥
English: Uh... Hey, Edgeworth.

Japanese: 窓のソト、コートをハタめかせて イトノコ刑事が落ちていくぞ！
English: Is that Detective Gumshoe out the window there? Oh no! He's falling to the ground!!!

Japanese: ‥‥そんなコトより、この子は 私の足元で、何をしているのだ。
English: Hold on. First let me see what this girl's doing crawling around my feet.

Japanese: （見もしない）
English: (He didn't even look.)

Japanese: あ！　こ、これは！ じ‥‥じ。じ‥‥‥
English: What!? "Letter of r-r-r-r..."

Japanese: 読めないのなら、教えてあげよう。 《じひょうとどけ》‥‥そう読む。
English: If you can't read, I'll read it for you.
It says, "Letter of Resignation."

Japanese: じ‥‥“辞表”？ 御剣！　おまえ‥‥
English: "Resignation"!? Edgeworth, you don't mean...?

Japanese: どうやら‥‥ 少し、つかれたようだ。
English: I'm tired, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 私の中の“何か”が死んでしまった ‥‥そんな気がしている。
English: I feel as if... something inside me has died.

Japanese: でも！　御剣検事さんは、 なんにも悪くないのに‥‥
English: But Mr. Edgeworth! None of it is your fault!

Japanese: 自分が歩いてきた道は 私自身、よく知っている。
English: I know the path I've walked. You don't need to tell me.

Japanese: ‥‥しかし。それは必ずしも、 正しいものではなかったようだ。
English: And the path I've walked... hasn't been a just one.

Japanese: 私には、それがユルせないし‥‥ また、ユルされるべきでもない！
English: I can't forgive myself for what I've done...
and no one else should forgive me either.

Japanese: （こいつ‥‥ホンキだぞ！）
English: (Uh oh. I think he's serious!)

Japanese: な‥‥成歩堂さん！ なんとかしないと、検事さんが！
English: Mr. Wright! Please, you have to do something!

Japanese: （この、辞表‥‥ 何かに使えないか‥‥？）
English: (This Letter of Resignation... I wonder if I can use it for anything?)

Japanese: 《御剣の辞表》を ポケットにしまいこんだ。
English: Put Letter of Resignation in pocket.

Japanese: ‥‥イイワケは、無意味だ。
English: There's no excuse for what I've done.

Japanese: ２年前‥‥私は、ニセの証拠に よって、有罪判決を得た。
English: Two years ago, I used false evidence to obtain a guilty verdict.

Japanese: これは、ぬぐい去るコトの できない“ジジツ”なのだからな。
English: That's what it all breaks down to, and nothing I do can erase that fact.

Japanese: でも！　おまえは 知らなかったんだろう？
English: But you didn't know, did you?

Japanese: それが、“ねつ造”された 証拠品だった、なんて。
English: I mean, that the evidence was falsified.

Japanese: ‥‥警察局と検事局は、 ２つで１つなのだ。
English: The Police Department and the Prosecutor's Office share a bond of trust.

Japanese: その“信頼関係”がコワれたとき ‥‥我々は、すべてを失うだろう。
English: If that bond is broken, we stand to lose everything.

Japanese: 警察局のミスは、私のミスで‥‥ 担当検事である、私のセキニンだ。
English: The Police Department's error is my error, my responsibility as the prosecutor in charge.

Japanese: 理由など、モンダイにはならぬ。
English: That fact remains the same no matter what excuses I might have.

Japanese: 御剣検事さん‥‥
English: Mr. Edgeworth...

Japanese: 私は‥‥自分のシゴトに ホコリを持っている。
English: I take pride in my work.

Japanese: それなのに、なぜ！ ここまで、ココロが乱れる‥‥
English: So tell me why! Why has it all come to this...

Japanese: （やっぱり‥‥ショックは かくしきれないみたいだ）
English: (Even Edgeworth can't keep this kind of emotion bottled up...)

Japanese: 明日の法廷‥‥ おまえ、やれそうなのか？
English: Are you up for the trial tomorrow?

Japanese: フッ‥‥
English: Hmph.

Japanese: 去年の事件といい、 キミにシンパイされてばかりだな。
English: First last year's trial, and now this one. It seems all you do is worry about me.

Japanese: 正直‥‥歯がゆい。
English: To be honest, you're getting on my nerves.

Japanese: まさか！　担当検事を はずれる、なんてコトは‥‥
English: But Mr. Edgeworth! You can't just walk out on the trial...

Japanese: 明日は最終日だ。今さら、担当を 変えることなど、できない‥‥
English: Tomorrow is the last day. It's too late to change prosecutors.

Japanese: それが、上層部のホンネだろう。
English: I'll bet that's what my superiors are banking on.

Japanese: ２年前の、あの事件が‥‥ 今さら、私の足元をすくうとは。
English: I never thought that case would come back to haunt me like this.

Japanese: それは‥‥？
English: What do you mean?

Japanese: あの事件の証拠品リストだ。 たしかに、妙に少ない気がする。
English: That list of evidence. It seems too short.

Japanese: リストの大きさも‥‥ いつもの半分ほど、だしな。
English: Most lists... run twice as long.

Japanese: はんぶん‥‥ （そりゃ、たしかに少ないな）
English: It's only half as long as most lists? (That is odd...)

Japanese: 罪門　直斗検事が殺害されて‥‥ 私は、彼のかわりに法廷に立った。
English: After Neil Marshall was murdered,
I became prosecutor for that case.

Japanese: 捜査には加われなかったが、 与えられた証拠で、罪を立証する！
English: I may not have been part of the investigation,

Japanese: ‥‥当時は、そのコトしか アタマになかったよ。
English: but at the time there was only one thing on my mind:
I'd use the evidence I was given to prove the suspect guilty!

Japanese: そういえば‥‥そのころの 写真を見かけましたね。
English: Say, we just saw a picture taken around that time.

Japanese: （あの写真‥‥ 何か“違和感”があったっけ）
English: (That picture... Something seemed strange about it...)

Japanese: 最後に、もう一度‥‥事件が あった日のコトを聞かせてくれ。
English: Could you tell us again about what happened that day?

Japanese: ‥‥多田敷捜査官が 殺害された、あの日‥‥
English: The day Detective Goodman was murdered?

Japanese: おまえ、警察局の式典に 参加していたんだよな？
English: You were participating in a ceremony over at the station, right?

Japanese: 式典はスキではない。しかし‥‥ 欠席するわけには行かなかった。
English: I've never cared for ceremonies, but I had to attend that one.

Japanese: やっぱり‥‥コレ、ですか？
English: Because you were awarded this?

Japanese: 表彰される本人が 欠席するワケにも行くまい。
English: Those receiving awards can't exactly skip out on the ceremony.

Japanese: シゴトを午前中にかたづけて、 クルマで警察局へ向かったのだ。
English: I finished up at the office in the morning, then drove over to the Police Department.

Japanese: “シゴトをかたづけた”‥‥？
English: You, "finished up" at the office?

Japanese: ‥‥ああ。 つまらぬ事務シゴトだ。
English: Yes. Just odds and ends- clerical stuff.

Japanese: その日はもう、検事局に 戻るつもりはなかったからな。
English: I didn't plan on returning to the office that day.

Japanese: あの、たのみごとを されるまでは。
English: That is, until I was asked to take something back.

Japanese: “たのみごと”‥‥ですか？
English: Take something back?

Japanese: コイツだよ。
English: This.

Japanese: ああ。巌徒局長さんから たのまれたんでしたっけ、それ。
English: Oh yeah. Chief Gant asked you to hold onto that, didn't he?

Japanese: すでに、半年前。 解決した事件の証拠品だ。
English: Yes. It was a piece of evidence in a case that was closed half a year ago.

Japanese: これを、検事局に持って帰って ほしい‥‥そう言われたのだ。
English: He asked me to bring it back to the Prosecutor's Office.

Japanese: （たしか‥‥ きのうも聞いたな‥‥）
English: (That's the story we heard yesterday...)

Japanese: あの日、検事局に帰ってきたのは、 局長に言われたから、なんだな？
English: So you came back here to the Prosecutor's Office because the Chief asked you to?

Japanese: まあ‥‥そういうことになるが。
English: That's right.

Japanese: ムジュン‥‥《矛盾》と書く。 このコトバの由来は‥‥
English: This award originates from an ancient Chinese tale.

Japanese: ヤリのような武器《矛（ホコ）》と 防具である《盾（タテ）》だ。
English: In Chinese, the word "contradiction" is written with two characters:

Japanese: このコトバには、有名な物語が あるのだが‥‥知っているな？
English: the first means "halberd," and the second means "shield." Have you heard this story?

Japanese: あ、ああ。聞いたことはあるよ。 ‥‥ちょっと、ド忘れしたけど。
English: Me? Oh, uh... sure. Everyone knows that! Why don't you tell it though... for Ema's sake.

Japanese: ‥‥中国の、古い故事だ。
English: Very well.

Japanese: ムカシ、楚（そ）の国に、 ある武器商人がいた。
English: Long ago in the kingdom of Chu, there was an arms merchant.

Japanese: 王の前に出た彼は、 ２つの商品を取り出した。
English: One day, he presented the king with two items.

Japanese: １つ目は‥‥すべてを貫く《矛》。 どんな防具も貫く、最強の武器だ。
English: The first was a halberd he claimed could slice through any shield or armor.

Japanese: もう１つは、決して破れぬ《盾》。 どんな攻撃も防ぐ、最強の防具だ。
English: The second was a shield he claimed could withstand any weapon.

Japanese: ふうん‥‥‥アレ。
English: Hmm... Wait a minute.

Japanese: その証人の発言は、 アキラカにムジュンしているッ！
English: Those claims contradict each other!

Japanese: “その証人”ではない。 “楚の商人”‥‥だ。
English: Very perceptive. But then again, you've heard this story before, right?

Japanese: そのとおり。この《矛》と《盾》は 互いに、あり得ない存在だ。
English: Anyway, as you mentioned, the very descriptions of these items discredit them both.

Japanese: ‥‥王は、商人にたずねた。
『ならば、その《矛》で《盾》を 突いたら、どうなるのか？』と。
‥‥その商人は、 コトバを失ったという。
English: When the king pointed this out, the merchant was left speechless.

Japanese: そこから、《ムジュン》という コトバが生まれた。
English: And thus, the Chinese word for "contradiction" was born.

Japanese: あ！　もしかして。 《割れた盾》と《折れた剣》は‥‥
English: Oh, I see! So the "chipped shield" and "broken knife" symbolize...

Japanese: その賞品は‥‥ 楚の商品、というわけだ。
English: Precisely so. They symbolize the merchant's items.

Japanese: 中国の物語は、商人が答えに 詰まったところで終わっている。
English: The ancient tale ends with the merchant at a loss for words,

Japanese: しかし‥‥我々は、その先にある 《答え》を追求しなければならぬ。
English: but it's in our nature to pursue matters to their conclusion...

Japanese: その結果が‥‥たとえ、このように ミニクイものであっても、な。
English: even if it results in something as ugly as this.

Japanese: へええ。ベンキョウに なりましたね、成歩堂さん！
English: Wow. Thanks, Mr. Edgeworth! I learned something new today!

Japanese: ‥‥でも。ちょっと、 ヘンじゃないか？
English: That's funny...

Japanese: じゃあ、おまえの賞品には なぜ、《盾》しかないんだ？
English: If that's so, then why were you only given a shield?

Japanese: 私にも、よくわからない。
English: You'll have to ask Chief Gant.

Japanese: ２年前、巌徒局長の提言で、 《矛》は廃止されたようだ。
English: Two years ago, he had the "halberd" part of the award abolished.

Japanese: （‥‥巌徒局長が‥‥）
English: (Chief Gant...)

Japanese: 《検事局賞の盾》のデータを 書きなおした。
English: King of Prosecutors Trophy updated in the Court Record.

Japanese: ‥‥ムカシ、あこがれていた 時期があったものだ。
English: I once dreamed of being a defense attorney, a long time ago.

Japanese: え！　御剣検事さんが、 弁護士に‥‥？
English: What? You wanted to be a defense attorney, Mr. Edgeworth?

Japanese: しかし、進むべき道が目の前に のびている以上‥‥
English: Yet, a clear path is laid out before me.

Japanese: 今さら振りかえるわけには いかないのだろうな。
English: I have no time to look back on what might have been.

Japanese: 巌徒局長の部屋に、 こんな写真がかざってあったよ。
English: This picture was hanging on the wall in Chief Gant's office.

Japanese: ‥‥罪門　直斗検事、か。 将来がキタイされていたよ。
English: Prosecutor Neil Marshall... He had just started making a name for himself.

Japanese: 《検事・オブ・ザ・イヤー》を 受賞したときの写真のようだ。
English: Looks like this was taken when he received the King of Prosecutors trophy.

Japanese: あ。そういえば‥‥ 気になることがあるんですけど。
English: Speaking of that, there's something that bothers me.

Japanese: ‥‥なんだろうか。
English: Yes?

Japanese: 罪門検事さんが、 手に持っている“賞品”‥‥
English: The trophy Mr. Marshall is holding...

Japanese: 御剣検事さんのと、 ちょっと、ちがいますよね。
English: it's a little different than yours.

Japanese: あ、ああ。たしかに。
English: Yes, you're right.

Japanese: ‥‥そう。思い出したようだ。
English: I remember now.

Japanese: ？　何をだ？
English: Remember what?

Japanese: ２年前まで‥‥検事局賞の 賞品は、あのカタチが正式だった。
English: That was the official prosecutor trophy used until that time.

Japanese: モチロン‥‥それには、 ちゃんとした由来がある。
English: There's a story behind it.

Japanese: ゆらい‥‥？
English: A story?

Japanese: （ちょっと、キョーミがあるな） 聞かせてくれるか？　そのハナシ。
English: (Sounds interesting.) Would you mind telling it to us?

Japanese: カンタンなコトだがな。
English: It's simple, really.

Japanese: 《ムジュン》‥‥ それが、コタエだ。
English: "Contradiction." That's what the award's based on.

Japanese: いつまで置いておくんだよ。 せっかくもらったのに。
English: You know, it wouldn't hurt if you put this up somewhere, like on a shelf.

Japanese: ‥‥そんなものには、すでに なんのイミもない。
English: That has no meaning for me anymore.

Japanese: “すでに”‥‥って。
English: What do you mean, "anymore"?

Japanese: それは、去年の私の姿だ。 ‥‥振り返るコトに、何かイミが？
English: That's who I was last year. What good is it to dwell on the past?

Japanese: 問いかけられたよ。
English: He's asking me?

Japanese: ちょっぴり、ハラ立ちますね。
English: That makes me kind of mad.

Japanese: あの‥‥それより、あたし。 少し、気になるんですけど。
English: Actually, something's been troubling me about this shield.

Japanese: この《盾》‥‥ なんか、ちがいませんか？
English: Look... Do you notice anything different?
Different...?

Japanese: ほら。法廷記録に、もう１つ あるじゃないですか。《盾》が。
English: Yeah, don't you remember? The other shield in the Court Record?

Japanese: （見せてみるか‥‥ もう１つの《盾》を）
English: (I guess I'd better present this other shield...)

Japanese: まさか、成歩堂。 担当検事である、この私が‥‥
English: Come now, Mr. Wright. You can't seriously expect me to just sit down...

Japanese: キミと事件の話をしながら お茶を飲むとでも‥‥？
English: and chat with you about the case over a cup of tea.

Japanese: いやいや。これは 関係ないんだろ？　事件とは。
English: But this isn't related to the case, remember? You said so yourself.

Japanese: む‥‥むゥ、たしかに。
English: So I did...

Japanese: あの日、局長さんから たのまれていたんですよね？
English: Chief Gant gave this to you that day, didn't he?

Japanese: 警察局から、ここへ 持ってくるように、って。
English: He asked you to bring it here from the Police Department...

Japanese: あの局長‥‥
English: The Chief is a very competent man...

Japanese: ヤリ手なのだが、たまに イミがわからないことがある。
English: but sometimes he does things that don't make any sense.

Japanese: まあ。ヤリ手ではない上、 イミがわからないよりはイイが。
English: At least it's better than being incompetent and doing things that don't make sense.

Japanese: （ある刑事さんのカオを 思い浮かべたようだ）
English: (A certain detective comes to mind...)

Japanese: 今回の事件‥‥何から何まで、 私を疑わしく見せているようだ。
English: It seems everything in this case is designed to cast doubt on me.

Japanese: ぼくをニラまないでほしいな。
English: Hey, don't look at me.

Japanese: 事件があった日‥‥たしかに、 私は証拠保管室に入った。
English: Yes, I went into the evidence room on the day of the crime.

Japanese: 巌徒局長から、急に たのまれたものでね。
English: Chief Gant asked me to do something.

Japanese: 《半年前に解決した事件の証拠を 検事局に持ち帰ってほしい》‥‥
English: To take evidence from a case solved six months ago back to the Prosecutor's Office...

Japanese: あそこには、私自身の 保管庫が用意されているのだ。
English: I have a locker in there as well.

Japanese: そのとき、保管室のようすは？
English: Did you notice anything amiss when you were in the evidence room?

Japanese: ‥‥あの部屋は、いつも暗い。
English: That room's always dark and I was in a hurry.

Japanese: 私も急いでいたし、 特に気になることはなかったな。
English: I didn't see anything out of the ordinary.

Japanese: “火のないところに ケムリは立たない”‥‥
English: They say where there's smoke, there's fire.

Japanese: どうやら‥‥そのケムリに巻かれて 真実を見失っていたようだ。
English: Apparently I was so caught up in the smoke, I lost sight of the truth.

Japanese: 御剣‥‥
English: Edgeworth...

Japanese: 彼らは正しかった。私には‥‥ 検事席に立つ資格など、ない。
English: The others are right. I have no right to serve as a prosecutor.

Japanese: でも！　検事さんは、自分の シゴトをしただけじゃないですか！
English: But Mr. Edgeworth! You were only doing your job!

Japanese: 私のなすべきコトは、つねに 私自身が決めてきた。
English: I've always made my own decisions about what I can or cannot do.

Japanese: 今回のコト‥‥ 自分をユルすことはできない。
English: That hasn't changed. Forgiving myself... is something I cannot do.

Japanese: （こいつ‥‥ホンキだぞ！）
English: (Uh oh. I think he's serious!)

Japanese: ‥‥成歩堂さん！ なんとかしないと、検事さんが！
English: Mr. Wright! Please, you have to do something!

Japanese: （この、辞表‥‥ 何かに使えないか‥‥？）
English: (This Letter of Resignation... I wonder if I can use it for anything?)

Japanese: まさか、成歩堂。 担当検事である、この私が‥‥
English: Wright, please. I'm the prosecutor on this case!

Japanese: キミと事件の話をしながら お茶を飲むとでも‥‥？
English: You don't expect me to sit here and discuss the case with you over a cup of tea!

Japanese: いや。お茶はいいんだ。 事件のハナシだけを‥‥
English: I'll pass on the tea. Just tell me about the case...

Japanese: 成歩堂さん！　御剣検事さんは イキに断ってるんです！
English: Mr. Wright! Mr. Edgeworth just told you "no" in a very stylish manner!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (Whose side are you on, anyway?)

Japanese: （本当に必要なものだけ 鼻先につきつけてやろう）
English: (Maybe if I just show him my best evidence I can get some reaction out of him.)
